content:
- Completed story-12 task 02: the default long-lane E2E path now runs through one runner container, runtime-shape assertions are typed, and source-bootstrap rendering no longer shells out to nested Cargo from inside tests.
- All required verification lanes passed: `make check`, `make test`, and `make test-long` (with `make lint` equivalent to `make check`). Updated the task markdown to `<status>completed</status> <passes>true</passes>`, checked the plan boxes, and ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh`.
- Next, stage all changed files including `.ralph`, commit with the task-finished message summarizing the container-runtime refactor and green lanes, push, then stop.

- Read the Choose-Task progress log, refreshed Ralph task summaries, loaded the chooser rules, and inspected the active failing tasks plus their task files for blockers and priority tags.
- Confirmed the actual repository shape is a Rust 2024 workspace with `crates/runner`, `crates/source-bootstrap`, a `Makefile` wired to `cargo clippy` and `cargo test`, and a README-driven Docker workflow; there are no active failing bug tasks, no visible `<passes>meta-task</passes>` items, and no visible `<blocked_by>` or extra `<priority>` tags on the current active candidates.
- The most logical next task is `.ralph/tasks/story-12-verify-e2e-integrity/03-task-assert-no-post-setup-source-commands-in-e2e.md` because story-12 tasks 01 and 02 already pass, this keeps the verification chain contiguous, and it directly validates the just-established single-container runtime boundary before moving on to novice-user docs/reporting or CI pipeline work. After writing `.ralph/current_task.txt`, quit immediately.

- Created `.ralph/current_task.txt` because it was missing and wrote the selected active task path into it.
- Selected `.ralph/tasks/story-12-verify-e2e-integrity/03-task-assert-no-post-setup-source-commands-in-e2e.md` as the next task under the chooser rules.
- Next agent should execute that task and then re-run the required validation lanes before marking progress.

- Read prior progress, current task pointer, and the required `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions.
- Confirmed the active task is `.ralph/tasks/story-12-verify-e2e-integrity/03-task-assert-no-post-setup-source-commands-in-e2e.md`; next I am loading that task file to determine whether this turn is planning or execution.
- If the task or its plan still says `TO BE VERIFIED`, I need to create or refine a plan under the story-specific plans directory, switch it to `NOW EXECUTE` only when the design is sound, then quit immediately.

- Determined task 03 is in planning state because the task markdown had no `<plan>` tag and no execution marker.
- Read story-12 plans 01 and 02 plus the current E2E support code. The main boundary smell for task 03 is that bootstrap source commands are audited, but post-bootstrap source SQL goes through a separate broad `execute_source_sql` escape hatch with no typed audit.
- Next I am adding a concrete task-03 plan under the story-12 plans directory, linking it from the task markdown, and ending the plan with `NOW EXECUTE` so the next turn can start the red/green implementation loop immediately.

- Created and linked the task-03 execution plan: `.ralph/tasks/story-12-verify-e2e-integrity/03-task-assert-no-post-setup-source-commands-in-e2e_plans/2026-04-19-no-post-setup-source-commands-plan.md`.
- The plan ends with `NOW EXECUTE` and focuses the boundary cleanup on one typed post-setup source-command audit, because the current smell is that bootstrap commands are logged but later `execute_source_sql` calls bypass that audit completely.
- Next turn should execute the plan via red/green TDD: centralize audited source command issuance, add typed post-setup integrity assertions, narrow the raw source SQL escape hatch, and only then run `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long`.

- Read prior progress, `.ralph/current_task.txt`, and the required `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions.
- Confirmed the active task is `.ralph/tasks/story-12-verify-e2e-integrity/03-task-assert-no-post-setup-source-commands-in-e2e.md`, and prior progress says its linked plan already ends in `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Next I am reading the task markdown, the linked execution plan, and the current source-command/E2E code so I can start the red-green loop without drifting from the approved design.

- Read the task markdown, linked `NOW EXECUTE` plan, and the main E2E support files.
- Confirmed the current smell exactly matches the plan: bootstrap source commands are wrapper-audited, but post-setup source SQL still escapes through `CdcE2eHarness::execute_source_sql()` and a separate `MultiMappingHarness::execute_source_sql()` path.
- Next I am tightening the first RED slice around repository contract coverage, then I will add the minimum typed source-command audit and reroute all source command execution through it before moving to the long-lane proof.

- Added the first RED/green contract for typed post-setup source-command auditing, then implemented `SourceCommandAudit`, `SourceCommandPhase`, and `PostSetupSourceAudit` in `crates/runner/tests/support/e2e_integrity.rs`.
- Rerouted post-setup source SQL through the Cockroach wrapper-backed audited path, removed the separate raw multi-mapping helper path, and exposed `post_setup_source_audit()` from the default bootstrap harness.
- Strengthened the default long-lane live-update test to assert post-setup source activity stayed workload-only, then fixed two real parser issues exposed by RED: bootstrap uses `--execute`, and multi-line SQL needs escaped wrapper-log encoding.
- Next I am running repository validation lanes (`make check`, `make test`, `make test-long`) and then I will update the task, plan checkboxes, and task-switch/commit steps only if every required lane is green.
